ES1! · CME · PLAN FOR JUL 06/07, 2026 · BUILT MON JUL 06 ~18:55 ET

ES Trading Plan — Mon ETH & Tue RTH

ES anchored to the completed Mon Jul 06 RTH: open-test-drive trend day, O 7,564.75 → early test L 7,552.50 → one-time-frame drive through ON high 7,574.25 → H 7,602.50 weak high under old POC 7,606.25 → C 7,593.50. Monday value migrated fully higher: VAL 7,575.00 · POC 7,584.75 · VAH 7,600.50. Daily trend remains constructive above the defended 20-EMA 7,520.25, with ATH 7,694.75 as the terminal upside magnet. Master question for Globex and Tuesday RTH: can buyers force acceptance through the 7,600.50–7,606.25 shelf before Wednesday 14:00 ET FOMC minutes?

RTH Close 7,593.50 · upper-range close Mon value VAL 7,575.00 · POC 7,584.75 · VAH 7,600.50 Decision shelf 7,600.50–7,606.25 Structure Buying Tail 7,558.50 · Weak High 7,602.50 Daily support 20-EMA 7,520.25 · ATR 96.25 Upside magnets 7,621.75/7,623.50 → 7,648.75 → ATH 7,694.75 ETH Bias 40% Bull / 50% Neutral / 10% Bear Tue RTH Bias 55% Bull / 27% Neutral / 18% Bear Event risk Wed 14:00 ET FOMC minutes
POST-CLOSE RE-ANCHOR (Mon Jul 06, 17:35 ET) — trend-day migration into the 7,600.50–7,606.25 shelf Monday's RTH was an open-test-drive trend day: opened 7,564.75 above Thursday's value, tested 7,552.50 in the first half-hour (held above the 7,549.25 poor low), then drove one-time-frame up through the ON high 7,574.25 to 7,602.50, closing 7,593.50. The session left a Buying Tail 7,558.50, a Weak High 7,602.50 stopped exactly under the old POC 7,606.25, and shifted the entire value area above Thursday's — the strongest one-day migration signal. Semis led the cash tape (AMD +6.6%, Dow record 53,056) and VIX fell to multi-week lows on an up day: orderly risk-on, not chase. Master variable: whether momentum can force acceptance through the 7,600.50–7,606.25 shelf on a no-data Tuesday before Wednesday 14:00 ET FOMC minutes (Warsh's first; forward guidance removed; inflation still 4.2%, so a hawkish-read risk sits 24h out). The plan: acceptance above 7,606.25 on a 30-min close validates the weak-high repair and opens 7,621.75 fib / 7,623.50 prior VAH, then the 7,648.75 poor-high magnet. Failure/rejection at the shelf rotates back through POC 7,584.75 to VAL 7,575.00–7,574.25; only acceptance below 7,575 loses the day's floor toward the 7,558.50 tail and the 7,549.25/7,550.50 cluster. ETH bias: 40% Bull / 50% Neutral / 10% Bear — migration + VIX say drift-up, but a shelf this defended usually needs RTH volume to break. MOC data not provided — unweighted.

01Top-Down Analysis — Overnight (Mon 18:00 → Tue 09:30 ET)

Anchored to Monday Jul 06 RTH CLOSED. The overnight reads against Monday's freshly-built profile (7,575.00 / 7,584.75 / 7,600.50) until Globex develops its own value. All values pulled from tonight's charts — nothing carried.

(a) Daily — MA stack, peaks & valleys

ES1! Daily · 20-EMA 7,520.25 tested and reclaimed last week · ATH 7,694.75 · ATR 96.25 · volume peaks/valleys on right axis

Why the 20-EMA at 7,520.25 mattersThe Jul 2 NFP flush (57k vs 117k est.) drove ES to 7,479.50 — through the 20-EMA intraday — and it was bought back the same session, closing above. That is a tested-and-defended dynamic support: the market showed you exactly where responsive institutional buyers live. As long as daily closes hold above 7,520, every intraday selloff is a dip-buy locator, not a trend change. A daily close below it would be the first mechanical crack in this leg.

Narrative: the daily is a textbook bull stack — all five EMAs ascending in order, price one ATR off the ATH after reclaiming the 20-EMA. Momentum has re-engaged post-holiday (three-day weekend gap-up behavior, semis leading). The only daily-scale caution is proximity: 7,694.75 is a full measured target overhead, and the last approach to it in mid-June was rejected hard enough to build the poor highs at 7,648.75/7,674.00 that still sit unrepaired between here and there.

(b) 5-min price-action recap — Monday Jul 06 RTH (open type: Open-Test-Drive ↑)

ES1! 5-min · ON high 7,574.25 / ON low 7,538.25 · the 09:30 test to 7,552.50 then a one-time-frame drive to 7,602.50

Open (09:30): ES opened 7,564.75 — above Thursday's VAH 7,544.00 (outside value, inside range: a look-above open). The first rotation was a test down to the session low 7,552.50, which held cleanly above both the 7,550.50 drawn line and the 7,549.25 prior poor low — responsive buyers defended the shelf on the first touch. 09:45–11:00: the drive. Initiative buying took out the red 7,565.00 line, then the Sunday-night ON high 7,574.25 without a fight — a classic open-test-drive signature that converts a look-above open into trend continuation. Price one-time-framed through 7,578.25 and 7,591.50 into late morning. ~12:30: the only real pullback of the day — a rotation back to the 7,578.25 shelf (former resistance) that was bought on the first test, confirming the level flip. 13:00–14:45: the afternoon leg to the 7,602.50 high, stalling to the tick under the old POC 7,606.25 — sellers showed up exactly where the last acceptance zone from the mid-June decline sits. Close: a modest MOC-hour dip to ~7,590 was absorbed; RTH closed 7,593.50 and ETH immediately ticked back to 7,600.25.

What the tape saidEvery dip Monday was bought at a pre-defined structural level (7,552.50 at the poor-low shelf, 12:30 at 7,578.25), and the only supply that mattered appeared at the old POC 7,606.25. That is initiative-buyer control with one clearly-defined ceiling. The order-flow question for tonight and Tuesday is singular: does that ceiling get repaired (accepted through) or defended a second time?

(c) Market Profile — exact data & structure

Shape & features (Mon Jul 06): elongated trend profile with a Buying Tail at 7,558.50 (single-print buying excess at the base — aggressive responsive buyers), value built in the upper half, and a Weak High at 7,602.50 — the day ended with a flat, unfinished top pressed against the old POC 7,606.25. Prior structural features still open on the map: Poor Low 7,549.25 (Jul 1) just under Monday's low, Poor Low 7,479.50 + VAL 7,482.50 from the NFP flush, and above: old POC 7,606.25, prior VAH 7,623.50, Weak High 7,636.75, Poor High 7,648.75, POC 7,666.50, Poor High 7,674.00, POC 7,682.75, Weak High 7,694.50 at the ATH.

STRUCTURE MAP (current 7,600.25) 7,694.50/7,694.75 Weak High + ATH ← final magnet 7,682.75 old POC 7,673.50/7,674.00 drawn + POOR HIGH ← repair magnet #2 7,666.50 old POC 7,648.75/7,654.50 POOR HIGH + drawn ← repair magnet #1 7,636.75 Weak High 7,632.25 drawn 7,621.75/7,623.50 fib extend + prior VAH ← first target above the shelf ──────── 7,600.50–7,606.25 VAH + WEAK HIGH 7,602.50 + 7,605.50 + old POC — THE SHELF ── ◄ price 7,600.25 7,591.50 drawn 7,584.75 TODAY POC ← first magnet below 7,574.25/7,575.00/7,578.25 ON high + VAL + drawn — value floor cluster 7,565.00 drawn (red) 7,558.50 BUYING TAIL ← initiative-buyer footprint 7,549.25/7,550.50 POOR LOW + drawn ← must-hold shelf 7,538.25 ON low (Sun) 7,527.50 drawn 7,520.25 20-EMA (daily) ← trend line-in-sand 7,508.25 drawn 7,501.25 Thu POC 7,479.50/7,482.25/7,482.50 POOR LOW + drawn + Thu VAL ← unrepaired NFP low
Why the 7,600.50–7,606.25 shelf is THE levelFour independent references stack within six points: today's VAH (where Monday's acceptance ended), today's weak high 7,602.50 (an unfinished auction that *wants* to be repaired), a drawn line at 7,605.50, and the old POC 7,606.25 — the highest-volume price of the entire mid-June distribution. Old POCs act as memory: the last time the market spent real time here, it was on the way down. Acceptance back above it means every seller from that June acceptance zone is underwater and the weak high repairs into open air (nothing but the 7,621.75/7,623.50 cluster until the poor highs). Rejection means the shelf worked twice, and two-sided rotation back through 7,584.75 becomes the path of least resistance.
Why the Buying Tail 7,558.50 and Poor Low 7,549.25/7,550.50 matterThe tail is proof of initiative buyers willing to lift offers below 7,560 — a footprint, not a prediction. The poor-low cluster just beneath it is the opposite kind of level: unfinished business that auctions like to revisit. Together they define tonight's real risk line: a probe into 7,549–7,558 that gets *bought* is the highest-quality long entry on the map (look-below-and-fail at a defended shelf); acceptance *below* 7,549.25 repairs the poor low, deletes the floor, and opens the void to 7,527.50/7,520.25.

(d) VIX & macro regime

VX1! closed 17.19, down again and at the low of a two-week slide from 18.45; spot VIX printed ~15.8 into the weekend and fell further Monday. Volatility falling on an up day is confirmation, not divergence — this is orderly risk-taking, consistent with the record Dow close (53,056) and semiconductor leadership (AMD +6.6%, Broadcom +3.7%). Macro regime: the weak Jul 2 NFP (57k vs 117k est., unemployment 4.2%) removed near-term hike fear under new Fed chair Warsh, who held at 4.25–4.50% in June. The counterweight: inflation is still running ~4.2%, and Wednesday 14:00 ET brings the first Warsh-era FOMC minutes with forward guidance stripped from the statement — the one scheduled event that could reprice the "no-hike" assumption. Tuesday itself is a light-calendar momentum day (SpaceX joins the NDX; Samsung prelim Q2 overnight into Wednesday keeps semis in focus). MOC imbalance data was not provided for Monday — unweighted in this plan.

03Context Summary — where the overnight starts

ES1! 30-min RTH+ETH · Jul 1 balance → Jul 2 NFP flush to 7,479.50 → holiday → Mon trend-up into the 7,600.50–7,606.25 shelf
FactorRead
Prior day type (Mon Jul 06)TREND UP open-test-drive; one-time-frame higher from 10:00
Settlement locationUpper 18% of range — 7,593.50 close, inside VA above POC, ETH already back at 7,600.25
Profile shapeElongated trend profile with Buying Tail 7,558.50 and Weak High 7,602.50
Value migrationFULLY HIGHER — today's VA (7,575–7,600.50) shares zero overlap with Thursday's (7,482.50–7,544); strongest migration print available
Structural features openWeak High 7,602.50 (repair candidate ↑) · Poor Lows 7,549.25 and 7,479.50 (repair candidates ↓) · Poor Highs 7,648.75 / 7,674.00 overhead
Price vs valueAT VAH — 7,600.25 sits on 7,600.50; +15.5 above POC, +25.25 above VAL
Extension riskMODERATE — only 15.5 pts above POC (fine) but +80 above the 20-EMA (0.83×ATR); room exists, chase risk grows above 7,624
Daily MA contextFull ascending stack; 20-EMA 7,520.25 tested & defended Jul 2
ATR (daily)96.25 — a normal day from 7,600 reaches 7,648–7,696 up or 7,552–7,504 down
CatalystTue = light calendar; FOMC minutes Wed 14:00 ET (Warsh's first); Samsung prelim Q2 + SpaceX NDX add keep semis/megacap flows active
Internals / flowsSemis led Monday (AMD +6.6%); Dow record; VIX falling on an up day = orderly risk-on; MOC not provided (unweighted)
Gap-open pre-read for Tuesday (Step-4.5 lookup)ES sits at the VAH. If Globex holds 7,584–7,606 overnight, Tuesday opens inside or at the edge of Monday's value → balance rules: fade the extremes back to 7,584.75 until a VA break. If Globex accepts above 7,606.25, Tuesday opens beyond Monday's range; measure the gap vs ATR — under ~30 pts (0.3×ATR) treat as a near-value open with look-above-and-fail risk, over ~48 pts (0.5×ATR) with the trend = gap-and-go, don't fade. A gap UP extends Monday's strong close with the close-location signal — continuation odds rise, gap-fill odds fall. A gap DOWN against Monday's strength would be counter-signal and favors a fill back to 7,593.50 unless the first 30 min holds below 7,575.

04What-If Scenarios — Overnight (E1–E6)

Scoped Mon 18:00 → Tue 09:30 ET against Monday's profile. Overnight liquidity is thin; the shelf at 7,600.50–7,606.25 usually needs RTH volume to genuinely repair — treat Globex breaks with suspicion until they hold 30-min closes.
E1
Balance on the shelf — 7,575 × 7,606 overnight rotation
BASE · 35%
▶
Zone
7,575.00 – 7,606.25 (Monday's upper value)
Context
After a trend day that closed pressing a four-source shelf, the statistically favored overnight is inventory-neutral rotation: longs are comfortable, shorts have no trigger, and Tuesday has no data to front-run. Value was just rebuilt 7,575–7,600.50 — Globex tends to auction inside fresh value ahead of a light-calendar cash open.
Setup
Fade the edges at quarter size: buy 7,576–7,578 (VAL/ON-high cluster) stop below 7,571, target 7,584.75 POC then 7,591.50; sell 7,603–7,605 stop above 7,609, target 7,591.50/7,584.75. R:R ≈ 1:1.5–2 per rotation.
Confirm
Sub-7-pt 30-min bars; both edge tests rejected on first touch; VIX futures flat.
Invalidate
A 30-min close outside 7,575/7,606.25 → rolls into E4/E5 (below) or E2 (above).
Probability
★★★★ 35% — defended: zero-overlap value migration means the market just agreed on new value; overnight sessions after trend days re-auction that value ~2/3 of the time absent a catalyst, and Tuesday's calendar is empty.
E2
Globex shelf break — drift-repair to 7,621.75
L 7,607 → 7,621+ · 30%
▶
Zone
7,606.25 → 7,621.75/7,623.50
Context
Weak highs left at the close frequently repair in the very next session, and thin overnight books make it cheap: with Asia leaning on Monday's semi momentum (Samsung prelim Q2 due, SpaceX NDX add Tuesday), a modest bid lifts the 7,602.50 weak high and old POC 7,606.25 without needing size. Above the shelf there is no profile reference until the 7,621.75 fib / 7,623.50 prior-VAH cluster — an air pocket that pulls.
Setup
Buy the retest: after a 30-min close above 7,606.25, bid 7,604–7,606 (shelf becomes support), stop 7,598.75 (back inside value = failed break), T1 7,614, T2 7,621.75, runner 7,623.50. R:R ≈ 1:2.5. Quarter size (ETH ceiling).
Confirm
7,606.25 holds as support on the first retest; ES leads NQ or moves with it; VIX futures unchanged/lower.
Invalidate
Re-acceptance below 7,600.50 → the break was a look-above-and-fail → rolls into E3.
Probability
★★★ 30% — defended: migration direction, falling VIX, and a weak (unfinished) high all point up; capped below E1 because old POCs from major distributions are rarely repaired on Globex volume alone.
E3
Look-above-and-fail at the shelf → rotate to POC
S 7,600 → 7,585 · 15%
▶
Zone
Probe 7,603–7,608, fail, rotate to 7,584.75
Context
The shelf already rejected price once (14:30 Monday, to the tick under 7,606.25). If Globex pokes above the weak high and finds no follow-through buying — the classic thin-book stop-run — the failed auction traps late longs and the rotation back to the day's fairest price (POC 7,584.75) is mechanical.
Setup
Short the failure: after a probe above 7,602.50 that closes (30-min) back below 7,600.50, sell 7,598–7,600, stop 7,607.25 (above the probe high), T1 7,591.50, T2 7,584.75. R:R ≈ 1:2.
Confirm
Probe high forms in <2 bars with declining volume; delta flips negative on the re-entry into value.
Invalidate
The "failure" re-bids and closes above 7,606.25 → rolls into E2 (stop and reverse only with confirmation).
Probability
★★ 15% — defended: the shelf's one-touch rejection record supports it, but everything else (migration, VIX, tape leadership) argues failures get bought at POC rather than cascading — hence the modest target.
E4
Value-floor dip-buy — 7,574.25–7,578.25 cluster test
L 7,575 → 7,592 · 10%
▶
Zone
7,574.25 – 7,578.25 (ON high + VAL + drawn)
Context
A normal overnight inventory correction after a trend day frequently checks back to VAL. Here VAL 7,575.00 stacks with the old ON high 7,574.25 (prior breakout point — support by polarity) and the 7,578.25 drawn shelf that was bought at 12:30 Monday. First touch of a triple-stacked, previously-defended cluster is the best long location on the overnight map.
Setup
Bid 7,575–7,577, stop 7,569.75 (below the 7,571 area = value lost), T1 7,584.75, T2 7,591.50, runner 7,600. R:R ≈ 1:2–3.
Confirm
Responsive buying on first touch (long lower wicks, delta flip); VIX futures not spiking.
Invalidate
30-min acceptance below 7,574 → value is failing → rolls into E5.
Probability
★★ 10% — defended: it requires a ~25-pt overnight give-back that neither the VIX trend nor the close-location signal predicts, but it is the standard "healthy" pullback path and the entry quality justifies pre-staging it.
E5
Tail & poor-low probe — look-below-and-fail at 7,549–7,558
L 7,551 → 7,575 · 7%
▶
Zone
7,549.25 – 7,558.50 (buying tail → poor-low/drawn cluster)
Context
If value fails overnight (Asia risk-off headline, semi reversal), the auction's natural destination is the unfinished business below: the 7,558.50 buying tail gets tested for conviction and the 7,549.25 poor low sits 3 pts under Monday's actual low — a magnet begging for repair. In a bull regime with a defended 20-EMA far below, this probe is a dip-buy locator, not a short: repair-the-poor-low-and-reverse is the play.
Setup
Bid the reclaim: let 7,549.25 trade, buy the recovery through 7,553 (stop 7,545.75 under the repair low), T1 7,565, T2 7,575, runner 7,584.75. R:R ≈ 1:3. No knife-catching — the reclaim is the trigger.
Confirm
Fast rejection wick at/under 7,549.25; buying tail zone holds on the retest.
Invalidate
30-min acceptance below 7,549.25 → floor deleted → rolls into E6.
Probability
★ 7% — defended: needs a ~50-pt overnight decline (0.5×ATR) against a falling-VIX tape; only a genuine headline gets it there, but the poor low's existence keeps it on the map.
E6
Risk-off shock — floor deleted, run at 7,527.50 / 20-EMA
S 7,547 → 7,522 · 3%
▶
Zone
Below 7,549.25 → 7,538.25 → 7,527.50 → 7,520.25
Context
The tail-risk branch: a geopolitical or Asia-session shock (the kind that hit over the Iran headlines in June) accepts price below the poor-low cluster, deleting Monday's floor. Below 7,549.25 the map is thin — ON low 7,538.25, then the 7,527.50 shelf, then the 20-EMA 7,520.25 where the whole leg's responsive buyers live. Even this branch ends at a buy zone.
Setup
Momentum short only on acceptance: sell 7,546–7,548 after a 30-min close below 7,549.25, stop 7,554.25, T1 7,538.25, T2 7,527.50, cover into 7,520–7,522. Quarter size, headline-driven tape.
Confirm
VIX futures gapping higher; NQ leading down; no immediate reclaim of 7,550.50.
Invalidate
Reclaim of 7,552.50 → failed breakdown → back to E5's long logic.
Probability
★ 3% — defended: nothing in VIX, migration, MA structure, or the calendar supports it; it exists because unhedged weekends/holiday re-entries occasionally deliver exogenous gaps.

RankScenarioDirectionProb★Key defense

4E4 Value-floor dip-buyBull (dip)10%★★Standard post-trend VAL check; triple-stacked cluster
5E5 Poor-low repair & reverseBear→Buy7%★7,549.25 magnet exists; needs a headline to reach
6E6 Risk-off shockBear3%★Pure tail risk; no supporting evidence tonight
Directional weight: Bull / constructive 40% (E2+E4) · Neutral 50% (E1+E3) · Bear 10% (E5+E6)
Base case, defended against the five pillars: rotation on the shelf with an upward-leaking bias. (1) Value migration — a zero-overlap shift higher is the single strongest bullish structure print; it argues any downside is corrective. (2) VIX — falling on an up day (VX1! 17.19, two-week low) confirms orderly risk-taking; there is no fear bid to fuel a real overnight flush. (3) MOC — not provided, unweighted. (4) MA stack — fully ascending with the 20-EMA tested and defended four sessions ago; the trend regime says treat all dips as locators. (5) Close location — settled in the upper 18% pressed against resistance, which historically leaks higher overnight but rarely forces a major Globex breakout through an old-POC shelf. What flips this read: a 30-min ETH close below 7,574 (kills the neutral-bull skew) or a VIX-futures spike above ~18 (kills the orderly-tape premise).

06Decision Map — Overnight

GLOBEX OPEN 18:00 ET @ ~7,600 (on the VAH / shelf)
│
├─ Holds 7,584.75–7,606.25 (most of the night)
│    └─ E1 BALANCE — fade edges to POC · reassess at EU open 03:00
│         ├─ EU bid lifts 7,606.25 → E2 — retest-buy → 7,614 → 7,621.75/7,623.50
│         └─ EU offers reject 7,603–7,606 → E3 — rotate to 7,584.75, buyers expected there
│

│         ├─ Holds 7,621.75 → carry gap-up into RTH → hand off to R1
│         └─ Fails back under 7,600.50 → trap → E3 rotation

          ├─ Reclaim > 7,553 → look-below-and-FAIL long (E5) → 7,575
          └─ Acceptance < 7,549.25 → E6 SHOCK → 7,538.25 → 7,527.50 → 20-EMA 7,520.25

Overnight disciplineQuarter-size ceiling all night (standing ETH rule). No new risk 08:00–09:30 ET Tuesday — let the cash open reprice the shelf. Tuesday has no scheduled print, so there is no flat-by rule tonight, but Wednesday 14:00 FOMC minutes means any ETH runner held into Tue RTH must be managed before Wednesday's session.

1

▶

After a 30-min ETH close above 7,606.25: bid 7,604.00–7,606.00 on the retest

Size vs ATRATR 96.25; overnight = quarter size maximum. A single ETH position risking >8–10 pts against a 96-pt daily range has no business being larger. Add nothing between 08:00–09:30 ET.

09Structural Warnings — Overnight

⚠️ Weak High 7,602.50 under old POC 7,606.25An unfinished top pressed against the highest-volume memory level of the June distribution. It WILL get resolved — either repaired (breakout fuel) or defended (double rejection). Do not sit passively short under it or passively long above it without the 30-min close confirming which.
⚠️ Poor Lows at 7,549.25 and 7,479.50 — open repair magnetsTwo flat, unfinished lows below. The near one sits 3 pts under Monday's low — a cheap overnight repair that will LOOK bearish for a few minutes and usually isn't (look-below-and-fail). The far one (NFP low) is the real regime floor; it only matters if 7,520 goes first.
⚠️ Buying Tail 7,558.50 — key S/R, single-print speedTails are single prints: thin acceptance. Price moving back into 7,552–7,558 travels FAST in both directions. Treat the zone as a decision area, not a resting-order shelf.
⚠️ Extension from POC / 20-EMAOnly +15.5 above today's POC (fine) but +80 above the 20-EMA (0.83×ATR). Mean-reversion risk is moderate and grows with every point above 7,624 — chase discipline: above the R2 cluster, longs are late.
ℹ️ Structural voidsAbove 7,606.25 → nothing until 7,621.75 (15-pt air pocket). Below 7,549.25 → thin to 7,538.25/7,527.50. Voids are no-trade zones mid-flight: enter at their edges, not inside them.
ℹ️ Event horizonTuesday RTH has no top-tier print; Wednesday 14:00 ET FOMC minutes (Warsh's first, guidance removed, CPI 4.2%) is the reprice risk. Post-holiday Tuesday books can be deceptively thin early.

7,549.25 – 7,575.00Below value, inside/at Monday's low — look-below-and-fail risk (gap AGAINST the close signal → fill odds high)Reversion candidate — R5 locator; failed probe = buy back to 7,575
< 7,549.25Beyond range down — counter-signal gap; something happened overnightOpen-drive down risk — R6; let IB decide, no knife-catching until 7,527/7,520

01Top-Down Analysis — Tuesday Jul 07 RTH

Cash-session plan for Tue Jul 07, 09:30–16:00 ET. Anchored to Monday's completed profile (7,575.00 / 7,584.75 / 7,600.50). Light data day; the session's job is to resolve the 7,600.50–7,606.25 shelf before Wednesday's FOMC minutes. Update the open-location branch with the actual overnight range at 09:00.

(a) Daily — MA stack, peaks & valleys

ES1! Daily · full ascending EMA stack · 20-EMA 7,520.25 defended on the Jul 2 NFP flush · ATH 7,694.75 one ATR overhead
MAValueDistanceStatus

Valley V (69.1K)7,276.75Volume void at the 100-EMA

Why the daily frame matters TuesdayWith the stack fully ascending and the 20-EMA freshly defended, the daily regime instructs the intraday one: bearish scenarios on this map are entry-locators for longs until proven otherwise. A genuine directional short requires a regime crack — practically, RTH acceptance below 7,549.25 with VIX confirming — and even then the first destination (7,520.25) is where the leg's buyers have already shown their hand once.

Narrative: Monday added a trend day on top of a defended pullback, the classic continuation sequence. The open question is purely tactical: the market must transact through the June rejection zone (7,606 → 7,694) that is littered with poor highs and old POCs. Those features are magnets AND friction — expect trending moves between them and fights at them.

(b) 5-min price-action recap — Monday RTH (open type: Open-Test-Drive ↑) and what it seeds for Tuesday

ES1! 5-min · Monday: 09:30 test to 7,552.50 → drive through ON high 7,574.25 → 12:30 pullback bought at 7,578.25 → 7,602.50 high under the old POC

Monday's cash session was an open-test-drive: a look-above open at 7,564.75 (above Thursday's VAH 7,544), one probing rotation down to 7,552.50 that held the 7,549.25/7,550.50 shelf, then a one-time-frame initiative drive through 7,565.00 and the ON high 7,574.25. The single midday pullback (≈12:30) stopped exactly at the 7,578.25 drawn shelf and was bought first-touch — a textbook polarity flip. The afternoon extension died at 7,602.50, one tick set under the old POC 7,606.25, and the MOC-hour dip to ~7,590 was absorbed into a 7,593.50 close. Seeds for Tuesday: (1) buyers defended every pre-mapped level first-touch — the dip-buy regime is active; (2) supply appeared at exactly one price zone, the shelf — Tuesday's auction opens with that as its unresolved question; (3) the weak high means Monday's buyers did NOT finish their auction — statistically that favors an early-session repair attempt; (4) Monday's low 7,552.50 never repaired the 7,549.25 poor low — both magnets below remain live.

Open-type read-throughTrend days that close on a weak high most often produce one of two Tuesday opens: a small gap-up that immediately tests the repair (trade it like R1 — the 7,606 retest is everything), or an in-value open that rotates first and attacks the shelf mid-morning (R2). A gap-down against Monday's strength would be counter-signal — historically those fill back to the prior close (7,593.50) unless the first 30 minutes accepts below VAL 7,575.

(c) Market Profile — exact data & structure

STRUCTURE MAP (reference 7,600.25) 7,694.00–7,694.75 ATH + Weak High ← terminal magnet 7,682.75 old POC 7,673.50/7,674.00 drawn + POOR HIGH ← repair magnet #2 7,666.50 old POC 7,648.75/7,654.50 POOR HIGH + drawn ← repair magnet #1 7,636.75 Weak High 7,632.25 drawn 7,621.75/7,623.50 fib + prior VAH ← first objective above the shelf ──────── 7,600.50–7,606.25 THE SHELF (VAH + Weak High + 7,605.50 + old POC) ── ◄ open decision 7,591.50 drawn 7,584.75 MON POC ← rotation magnet 7,574.25–7,578.25 ON high + VAL + drawn ← value floor cluster 7,565.00 drawn (red) 7,558.50 BUYING TAIL (single prints — speed zone) 7,549.25/7,550.50 POOR LOW + drawn ← must-hold 7,538.25 ON low · 7,527.50 drawn · 7,520.25 20-EMA 7,479.50–7,482.50 NFP POOR LOW + Thu VAL + drawn · 7,472.75 daily peak
Why the old POC 7,606.25 decides TuesdayPOCs are where the most two-sided business was done; an OLD POC overhead is the densest population of trapped June sellers and un-filled June buyers on the map. Trading through it on RTH volume converts that whole population into fuel (shorts covering, sidelined buyers chasing) — which is why the first target after acceptance (7,621.75/7,623.50) tends to arrive fast. Failing at it a second time tells responsive sellers their level works, and rotation back through the fresh value (7,584.75, then 7,575) becomes the day's business. There is no third option; that is what makes it the cleanest reference on the board.
Why the poor lows are Tuesday's hidden risk-reward7,549.25 sits 3 pts under Monday's low: any morning weakness that repairs it and reverses is a look-below-and-fail — historically one of the highest-win-rate longs in a bull migration regime. 7,479.50 (with Thu VAL 7,482.50 and the 1.373M daily peak at 7,472.75) is the structural floor of the whole pattern — it is NOT a Tuesday target unless the 20-EMA fails first, but its existence caps how aggressively you should press shorts: the market has unfinished downside business it can service later without breaking trend.

(d) VIX & macro

VX1! 17.19 and falling all week (18.45 → 17.15 low); spot ~15.8 and lower Monday. Declining vol on rising price = orderly institutional risk-adding, the opposite of a blow-off. Regime inputs for Tuesday: weak NFP (57k) has the tape trading "bad news = no hike" under Warsh; semis are the leadership (AMD +6.6% Monday, Samsung prelim Q2 overnight Tue→Wed, SpaceX joins the NDX Tuesday); Q2 earnings (FactSet +23% y/y est.) start next week. The binary risk is NOT Tuesday — it is Wednesday 14:00 ET FOMC minutes, the first of the Warsh era with forward guidance deleted and CPI still 4.2%: a hawkish read is the one scheduled thing that can flip this regime. Expect Tuesday afternoon positioning to lighten into it. MOC data not provided — unweighted.

03Context Summary — Tuesday RTH

ES1! 30-min RTH+ETH · the week's shape: Jul 2 NFP flush bought at the 20-EMA zone → holiday → Monday trend day into the shelf
FactorRead
Prior day typeTREND UP (open-test-drive), value shifted fully higher
Settlement location7,593.50, upper 18% of range, inside VA above POC; ETH holding ~7,600
Profile shapeElongated / trend — Buying Tail 7,558.50, Weak High 7,602.50
Value migrationHIGHER, zero overlap vs Thursday — initiative buyers repriced the whole market
Structural featuresWeak High 7,602.50 unresolved · Poor Lows 7,549.25 / 7,479.50 open · Poor Highs 7,648.75 / 7,674.00 overhead · old POCs 7,606.25 / 7,666.50 / 7,682.75
Price vs POC/VAH/VALAt VAH — +15.5 vs POC, +25.25 vs VAL
Extension riskMODERATE — POC distance trivial; 20-EMA distance +80 (0.83×ATR); real chase-risk begins above the R2 cluster (~7,624)
Daily MA contextFull bull stack, 20-EMA defended Jul 2
ATR / expected move96.25 — from a ~7,600 open, ±1 ATR spans 7,504–7,696; the whole overhead ladder is reachable on a trend day
CatalystLight Tuesday — no top-tier US data; SpaceX NDX inclusion; Samsung prelim Q2 overnight; FOMC minutes Wed 14:00 shadows the afternoon
Internals / flowsSemi leadership intact; Dow at records; VIX falling on up days; MOC not provided (unweighted)
Gap-open classification (Step-4.5 lookup, finalize at 09:29)Four branches: (i) open inside 7,575–7,600.50 → balance rules, fade extremes to 7,584.75 until a VA break; (ii) open 7,600.50–7,606.25 or small gap-up <~30 pts → look-above territory: the first 30-min decides repair (hold above 7,606.25 = go with it) vs fail (back under 7,600.50 = fade to POC); (iii) open gap-up >~48 pts (0.5×ATR) after an overnight breakout → gap-and-go: WITH Monday's strong close the fill odds collapse — buy the first hold of the open print, don't fade; (iv) open below 7,575 → gap against the close signal: fill-back-to-7,593.50 odds are high UNLESS the IB accepts below 7,575 — then the tail/poor-low sequence runs first. The close-location signal (strong close → gap up = continuation) is the tilt: any upside gap Tuesday is trend-confirming, any downside gap is suspect until proven.

04What-If Scenarios — Tuesday RTH (R1–R6)

Keyed to the open's location vs Monday's profile. One structural question rules them all: does RTH volume accept price through the 7,600.50–7,606.25 shelf, or does the shelf hold a second time?
R1
Shelf acceptance — weak-high repair, run the ladder to 7,621.75/7,623.50+
L 7,606 → 7,623+ · 30%
▶
Zone
Open ≥ ~7,598; acceptance above 7,606.25
Context
The trend-continuation branch. Monday's buyers left an unfinished high one tick under the old POC; a fresh cash session with semi leadership and no data in the way is exactly the volume that repairs it. Acceptance above 7,606.25 converts the June acceptance zone into fuel — trapped shorts and chasing longs — with a 15-pt air pocket straight to the 7,621.75/7,623.50 cluster and the 7,648.75 poor-high magnet beyond.
Setup
Buy the retest of the shelf-as-support: 7,604–7,606.50 after a 30-min close above 7,606.25 (or the first hold of the open print on a gap above it). Stop 7,598.75. T1 7,614, T2 7,621.75–7,623.50, runner 7,632.25 → 7,648.75. R:R ≈ 1:2.5–4.
Confirm
IB extends above 7,606.25 and holds it on the first pullback; NQ making session highs with ES; VIX flat-to-down.
Invalidate
30-min re-acceptance below 7,600.50 → the repair failed → rolls into R3.
Probability
★★★★ 30% — defended: value migration (zero overlap), close location (upper 18%), VIX trend, and MA stack all vote continuation; discounted from higher only because old-POC shelves reject first attempts more often than not and Wednesday's minutes argue for some afternoon restraint.
R2
POC dip bought — grind up, late shelf retest (P-continuation)
L 7,585 → 7,602+ · 25%
▶
Zone
Open inside value; morning dip to 7,584.75 (±7,591.50 first)
Context
The patient-bull branch. An in-value open rotates down to test whether Monday's fairest price attracts responsive buyers. In a fresh-migration regime it almost always does on first touch — the POC is where Monday's biggest volume agreed on price, and nothing overnight has invalidated that agreement. The afternoon then re-attacks the shelf with better fuel (morning shorts covering).
Setup
Bid 7,584.75–7,586.50 on the first morning test, stop 7,578.75 (under the 7,578.25 shelf), T1 7,591.50, T2 7,600.50, runner through the shelf if R1 triggers late. R:R ≈ 1:2.5.
Confirm
First-touch responsive buying at POC (delta flip, lower wick); value building overlapping-to-higher intraday.
Invalidate
30-min acceptance below 7,584.75 turns the POC into resistance → rolls into the VAL test (R5 locator via S3).
Probability
★★★ 25% — defended: the most common day-after-trend path is a value re-test then continuation; POC-first-touch holds run high win-rates in migration-up regimes, and the light calendar removes the usual morning-data ambush.
R3
Look-above-and-fail — second shelf rejection, rotate through value
S 7,599 → 7,578 · 15%
▶
Zone
Probe 7,603–7,610 fails → 7,584.75 → 7,575–7,578.25
Context
The responsive-seller branch. The shelf has one rejection on record (Monday 14:30). If Tuesday's open probes above 7,602.50, tags the 7,606.25 population, and the buying dries up — a failed auction at a known supply zone — the trapped breakout longs become the down-rotation's fuel. Destination logic: fairest price first (7,584.75), then the value floor, where the bull regime says buyers should reappear.
Setup
Short 7,597–7,600 after the probe closes (30-min) back below 7,600.50, stop above the probe high (~7,608–7,611), T1 7,591.50, T2 7,584.75, runner 7,578.25. R:R ≈ 1:2. Cover fully into S3 — do not press shorts into the value floor in this regime.
Confirm
Probe on declining volume; NQ diverging (not confirming the new high); delta negative on re-entry.
Invalidate
Re-bid closing above 7,606.25 → the "failure" was absorption → flip to R1.
Probability
★★ 15% — defended: the shelf's defense record and pre-minutes caution earn it real weight, but every macro pillar (migration, VIX, stack, close) says failures get bought at value rather than trending — hence a rotation target, not a trend target.
R4
Balance day — 7,575 × 7,606 rotation into the minutes
Fade edges · 12%
▶
Zone
Monday's value area, both edges respected all day
Context
The pre-event compression branch. With no Tuesday data and a major reprice risk 24 hours out, dealers and funds may simply refuse the shelf fight: an inside/balance day that re-auctions Monday's value at declining volatility, saving the directional decision for Wednesday 14:00. D-shaped profile, POC migrating little.
Setup
Responsive fades at the edges: sell 7,600.50–7,603 / buy 7,575–7,578.25, stops 6–7 pts beyond, target the POC 7,584.75 from both sides. Reduce size after the first two rotations complete. R:R ≈ 1:1.5 per rotation.
Confirm
IB contains both extremes; volume light; TICK/internals mixed; VIX drifting sideways.
Invalidate
Any 30-min acceptance outside 7,575/7,606.25 → hand off to R1 or R5.
Probability
★★ 12% — defended: event-eve balance is a real pattern, but Monday's tape showed initiative buyers unwilling to wait, and post-holiday re-engagement flows usually pick a direction — so this is a minority path.
R5
Value failure — tail test & poor-low repair, dip-buy locator
L 7,551 → 7,575+ · 12%
▶
Zone
Acceptance < 7,575 → 7,565 → 7,558.50 tail → 7,549.25/7,550.50
Context
The healthy-correction branch. If the value floor gives way (soft open, semi cool-off), the auction services its unfinished downside business: conviction-test the 7,558.50 tail, repair the 7,549.25 poor low. In this regime that sequence is a LOCATOR, not a trend: the daily stack, the defended 20-EMA below, and falling VIX all say the flush finds responsive buyers at the cluster. The reclaim after the repair is the trade.
Setup
No shorts into the tail (single-print speed cuts both ways). Long trigger: 7,549.25 prints, then reclaim through 7,553 — buy 7,552–7,554, stop 7,545.75, T1 7,565, T2 7,575, runner 7,584.75. R:R ≈ 1:3.
Confirm
Fast rejection at/under the poor low; VIX NOT spiking through ~17; breadth stabilizing on the reclaim.
Invalidate
30-min acceptance below 7,549.25 → floor deleted → rolls into R6.
Probability
★★ 12% — defended: requires giving back ~50 pts of a migration day, which close-location statistics disfavor; kept meaningful because the poor low is only 3 pts under Monday's low — cheap for the market to service even inside an up-day.
R6
Trend down — floor deleted, 20-EMA run (regime-crack risk)
S 7,547 → 7,522 · 6%
▶
Zone
< 7,549.25 accepted → 7,538.25 → 7,527.50 → 7,520.25 (→ 7,501.25 on extremes)
Context
The exception branch — it requires a fresh catalyst (hawkish minutes leak, AI-unwind sequel to Jul 2, geopolitical headline), because structure alone doesn't produce it: a genuine directional short day in this regime needs the macro story to change. Below the poor-low cluster the map is thin to the 20-EMA, where the leg's proven buyers wait; even the bear branch terminates at a buy zone unless the EMA itself fails.
Setup
Momentum short only on acceptance: sell 7,546–7,548 after a 30-min close below 7,549.25, stop 7,554.75, T1 7,538.25, T2 7,527.50, cover 7,520–7,522. Half size; flat if the catalyst is ambiguous. R:R ≈ 1:2.5.
Confirm
VIX +1 or more and rising; NQ leading down hard; failed reclaims of 7,552.50.
Invalidate
Reclaim > 7,552.50 → failed breakdown → revert to R5's long logic immediately.
Probability
★ 6% — defended: zero current evidence (VIX falling, migration up, stack rising, strong close) supports it; it carries weight only because Jul 2 proved this tape CAN flush 100 pts intraday when the AI complex wobbles.

05Probability Matrix — Tuesday RTH

RankScenarioDirectionProb★Key defense
1R1 Shelf acceptance → 7,623+Bull30%★★★★Migration + close location + VIX + weak-high repair statistics
2R2 POC bought → grind upBull25%★★★Day-after-trend value retest; first-touch POC defense
3R3 Look-above-and-failNeutral-bear15%★★Shelf's rejection record; pre-minutes caution
4R4 Balance into the minutesNeutral12%★★Event-eve compression pattern
5R5 Value fail → poor-low locatorBear→Buy12%★★Open repair magnets 3 pts under Monday's low
6R6 Trend down / regime crackBear6%★Needs a fresh catalyst; Jul 2 precedent only
Directional weight: Bull 55% (R1+R2) · Neutral 27% (R3+R4) · Bear 18% (R5+R6)
Base case, defended pillar by pillar: upward resolution of the shelf, most likely via R1 directly or R2's dip-first route. (1) Value migration — Monday's zero-overlap shift higher is initiative conviction; day-after statistics favor continuation or higher-value balance, not reversal. (2) VIX — a two-week decline accelerating into Monday's rally is institutions adding risk with cheap hedges; reversals rarely start from falling vol. (3) MOC — not provided; unweighted (flag: if Monday's MOC was a large buy imbalance, add ~5 pts of weight to R1; a large sell, shift it to R3). (4) MA stack — perfect ascending order with the 20-EMA battle-tested Jul 2; regime says dips are supply for buyers. (5) Close location — settling in the top fifth of a trend day pressed on resistance historically resolves higher within 1–2 sessions ~2 times in 3. The falsifier set: RTH acceptance below 7,575 kills the 55% bull weight; VIX reclaiming 18 kills the orderly-tape premise; a hawkish minutes leak flips the regime assumption itself. Position for Wednesday: whatever Tuesday decides, carry reduced exposure into 14:00 ET Wednesday.

06Decision Map — Tuesday RTH

TUE 09:30 ET OPEN — locate vs Monday's profile (V: 7,575.00 / 7,584.75 / 7,600.50)
│
├─ OPEN ABOVE 7,606.25 (shelf repaired overnight / gap-up)
│    ├─ Gap > ~48 pts (0.5×ATR) → GAP-AND-GO — don't fade; buy first hold of open print → 7,632 → 7,648.75
│    └─ Gap < ~30 pts → look-above test: first 30-min HOLDS > 7,606.25 → R1 buy 7,606 retest
│                        first 30-min FAILS back < 7,600.50 → trap → R3 rotate to 7,584.75
│
├─ OPEN 7,584.75–7,606.25 (upper value — most likely)
│    ├─ IB extends up, accepts > 7,606.25 → R1 — ladder to 7,621.75/7,623.50
│    ├─ Shelf probe fails (no acceptance) → R3 — short the failure, cover into value
│    └─ IB contains both edges → R4 BALANCE — fade 7,600.50×7,575, POC pivot
│
├─ OPEN 7,575.00–7,584.75 (lower value)
│    └─ First touch of POC 7,584.75 from below / VAL from above:
│         ├─ POC/VAL BOUGHT → R2 — grind up, afternoon shelf attack
│         └─ VAL LOST (30-min accept < 7,575) → value fail → tail test → R5 sequence
│
└─ OPEN BELOW 7,575 (gap against the close signal)
     ├─ Reclaims 7,575 in the first hour → look-below-and-FAIL — long → fill to 7,593.50
     └─ Holds below → 7,565 → 7,558.50 tail
          ├─ 7,549.25 repaired then reclaimed > 7,553 → R5 LOCATOR LONG → 7,575+
          └─ 30-min acceptance < 7,549.25 → R6 → 7,538.25 → 7,527.50 → 20-EMA 7,520.25

Level-by-level: holds vs breaks

LevelIf HOLDSIf BREAKS
7,648.75–7,654.50caps poor high defended — expect a sharp responsive fade; take trend-day profits in frontrepair #1 done next stop 7,666.50 → 7,674.00; trend-day extension toward the ATH zone

7,584.75 (POC)bought R2 confirmation — value intact, target the shelf againvalue weakening VAL test imminent; tighten longs, watch S3
7,574.25–7,578.25floor holds bull structure intact — dip-buy zone, stop under 7,571VALUE FAILED day-type change — tail/poor-low sequence engages (R5)
7,558.50 (tail)conviction confirmed responsive buy back toward VALtail erased Monday's initiative print deleted — 7,549.25 repair next

07Bias Flip Conditions — Tuesday RTH

Starting biasFlip toTriggerAction
Bull (55%)Bull-extended30-min acceptance > 7,606.25R1 engaged — buy the retest, trail under structure, ladder targets
BullNeutralShelf probe fails: 30-min close back < 7,600.50 after a >7,602.50 probeStand down on breakout longs; R3 fade to POC only with confirmation
NeutralBullFirst-touch POC 7,584.75 bought hardR2 — long the hold, target the shelf
NeutralNeutral-bear30-min acceptance < 7,575.00Value failed — no longs until the tail/poor-low sequence resolves (R5)
Neutral-bearBull (dip)7,549.25 repairs, reclaim > 7,553Locator long, stop 7,545.75 — the look-below-and-fail
Neutral-bearBear30-min acceptance < 7,549.25 + VIX risingR6 momentum short toward 7,527.50/7,520.25, half size
BearNeutralReclaim > 7,552.50Failed breakdown — cover instantly; breakdown shorts have no edge above the poor low
Session disciplineHalf size on the first trade of the day until the IB confirms a branch. Trend-day rules only after acceptance through 7,606.25 OR below 7,549.25 — everything between is rotation sizing. Reduce all exposure into the last hour: Wednesday 14:00 FOMC minutes is the next reprice, and Tuesday's close is the last clean exit before it.

08Tactical Playbook — Tuesday top 3

1
Shelf-acceptance retest long (R1) — the A+ trade
R:R 1:2.5–4
▶
Entry
After a 30-min RTH close above 7,606.25 (or first hold of a gap-up open print above it): bid 7,604.00–7,606.50
Stop logic
7,598.75 — back inside Monday's value voids the repair; no averaging below the VAH
Scaling
⅓ at 7,614.00, ⅓ at 7,621.75–7,623.50, runner trailed 8 pts under 30-min structure toward 7,632.25 / 7,648.75
Targets
7,614.007,621.75–7,623.507,632.25 → 7,648.75
Kill switch
Second failed retest of 7,606 or VIX +0.75 intraday → flat, downgrade to R3/R4 logic
2
POC first-touch long (R2)
R:R 1:2.5
▶
Entry
7,584.75–7,586.50 on the first morning test of Monday's POC
Stop logic
7,578.75 — under the 7,578.25 drawn shelf; a POC that trades through first-touch is telling you value is migrating down, not offering a discount
Scaling
½ at 7,591.50, ¼ at 7,600.50, runner joins R1 through the shelf if acceptance triggers
Targets
7,591.507,600.507,606.25+ (hand-off)
Kill switch
30-min acceptance below 7,584.75 → exit; next long consideration only at S3 or the R5 locator
3
Poor-low repair-and-reclaim long (R5 locator)
R:R 1:3+
▶
Entry
7,549.25 prints (repair) → buy the reclaim 7,552.00–7,554.00; the reclaim is the trigger, never the falling knife
Stop logic
7,545.75 — under the repaired low; if it re-breaks, R6 is live and longs are wrong by definition
Scaling
½ at 7,565.00, ¼ at 7,575.00, runner 7,584.75
Targets
7,565.007,575.007,584.75
Kill switch
VIX ripping / NQ making new lows on the reclaim attempt → stand down; locators need a stabilizing tape
Sizing frameATR 96.25. First trade half size until the IB votes. Full size only after one clean 30-min structure confirms the branch (e.g. 7,606 holding as support, or the poor-low reclaim holding 7,555). Nothing new after ~15:00 ET — Wednesday's minutes own the next session.

09Structural Warnings — Tuesday RTH

⚠️ The shelf is binary — don't trade the middle of the fight7,600.50–7,606.25 concentrates four references in six points. Positions initiated INSIDE 7,598–7,608 without acceptance evidence are coin-flips at the worst location on the map. Enter on the resolution (retest after acceptance, or the failure close), not the probe.
⚠️ Second-rejection risk at an old POCOld POCs defended once tend to be defended again before they break. Monday already produced one to-the-tick rejection at 7,606.25. A gap-up open that stalls without extending in the first 30 minutes is the R3 trap forming — watch NQ confirmation and delta before trusting any breakout print.
⚠️ Single-print speed zonesThe Buying Tail 7,558.50 and the air pocket above 7,606.25 (to 7,621.75) are thin. Price traverses them fast; stops inside them will slip. Rest orders at the edges (7,552/7,565; 7,606/7,621), not inside.
⚠️ FOMC-minutes shadow (Wed 14:00 ET)The first Warsh-era minutes with guidance removed and CPI at 4.2% is a genuine two-sided reprice risk. Expect Tuesday-afternoon de-risking flows; do not read a 14:00–16:00 fade Tuesday as trend information, and carry reduced size overnight.
⚠️ Extension discipline+80 above the 20-EMA already. R1 is a continuation trade at good location (7,606), not a chase at 7,625+. Above the R2 cluster, fresh longs carry the worst R:R of the ladder — let pullbacks come to you.
ℹ️ Unrepaired lows are patient magnets7,549.25 and 7,479.50 don't need to trade Tuesday — but they are why blind long-and-hold through a value failure is wrong even in a bull regime. Respect the flip levels.
ℹ️ MOC unweightedMonday's MOC imbalance wasn't provided. If you have it: large buy imbalance → upgrade R1; large sell → upgrade R3. Fold it in at the open.

10Session Transition — Tuesday close → Wednesday (FOMC minutes)

If Tuesday settles…Structure implicationWednesday setup
> 7,623.50Shelf AND first cluster repaired; value migrating againTrend continuation into the minutes — but event risk says carry light; 7,648.75 poor high is the magnet if minutes read dovish
7,606.25 – 7,623.50Weak high repaired, holding above the old POCConstructive — Wednesday opens with 7,606 as the line; minutes decide the extension
7,584.75 – 7,606.25Second rejection but value intact — coiled balanceCompression → event break — the minutes likely resolve the shelf; straddle logic, no directional lean pre-14:00
7,575.00 – 7,584.75Lower-value close; migration stallingVulnerable balance — a hawkish read attacks 7,558.50/7,549.25 immediately
7,549.25 – 7,575.00Value failed; tail/poor-low sequence in progressDefensive — Wednesday pre-minutes rallies are sells against 7,575 until reclaimed
< 7,549.25Floor deleted before the eventRisk-off regime test — 20-EMA 7,520.25 is the arbiter; minutes become an amplifier either way

Standing note: whatever Tuesday builds, Wednesday 14:00 ET re-anchors everything — rebuild the plan off Tuesday's completed profile in the morning and treat pre-minutes structure as provisional.

11Final Summary — Tuesday RTH

ItemRead
Macro biasBullish — ascending stack, defended 20-EMA, falling VIX, weak-NFP/no-hike regime, semi leadership
Intermediate biasBullish — zero-overlap value migration; ATH 94 pts up with poor highs as waypoints
Session biasBull 55 / Neutral 27 / Bear 18
Base caseR1: RTH volume repairs the 7,602.50 weak high, accepts above 7,606.25, ladders to 7,621.75/7,623.50 (R2's POC-dip-first route is the same destination)
A+ trade7,604–7,606.50 retest long after 30-min acceptance above 7,606.25 → 7,614 / 7,621.75+ (stop 7,598.75)
Best-location alternatePOC 7,584.75 first-touch long (stop 7,578.75)
Highest-R:R contrarian7,549.25 repair-and-reclaim locator long (stop 7,545.75)
Real short conditionsOnly R3 (confirmed shelf failure, cover into value) or R6 (acceptance < 7,549.25 with VIX rising, half size)
Key bull level7,606.25 — acceptance above converts the June supply into fuel
Key bear level7,575.00 — acceptance below fails Monday's value and opens the tail/poor-low sequence
Hard floor / regime line7,549.25 poor low → then 20-EMA 7,520.25
Biggest riskA stall-and-trap at the shelf (R3) morphing into afternoon de-risking ahead of Wednesday's minutes
Day-type expectationNormal-variation up through the shelf, or upper-value balance that resolves Wednesday — reduce into the close either way
ES Trading Plan — Mon Jul 06 ETH & Tue Jul 07 RTH · Compiled from attached unfinished HTML parts, embedded chart images, and delta-box notes.
